conditions.name: Erectile Dysfunction (ED)
conditions.what_it_is: Difficulty getting or keeping an erection firm enough for sex. It can be occasional or persistent.
how_severe_can_it_be: Mild: occasional difficulty, often stress/fatigue related.
Moderate: happens often and affects confidence/relationship.
Severe: consistently unable to get/maintain erection; sometimes linked with diabetes, blood pressure, hormone issues, or circulation problems.
red_flags: Chest pain, shortness of breath, or known heart disease
Sudden new ED after starting a new medicine
Painful erection, penile injury, or erection lasting >4 hours
suggested_treatments: Lifestyle: sleep, exercise, reduce alcohol/smoking, manage stress
Medical evaluation: blood sugar, BP, lipids, testosterone if needed
Counseling if anxiety/performance stress is a factor
questions_to_ask: How long has it been happening?
Is it occasional or most of the time?
Any diabetes, BP, thyroid issues, or new medications?

conditions.name: Premature Ejaculation (PE)
conditions.what_it_is: Ejaculating sooner than desired during sex, causing distress or difficulty in sexual satisfaction.
how_severe_can_it_be: Mild: happens sometimes, improves with techniques.
Moderate: happens frequently and causes significant stress.
Severe: happens almost every time and affects relationships and confidence.
red_flags: Pain, burning, discharge, or fever (possible infection)
Sudden onset with severe anxiety or depression
suggested_treatments: Behavioral techniques (start–stop, squeeze technique) and pelvic floor exercises
Treat underlying infections if symptoms suggest it
questions_to_ask: Is it lifelong or started recently?
Do you also have erection issues?

conditions.name: Sexually Transmitted Infection (STI/STD)
conditions.what_it_is: Infections that can spread through sexual contact. Some cause symptoms (burning, discharge, sores) and some can be silent.
how_severe_can_it_be: Some STIs are easily treatable with medication.
If untreated, some can cause complications like infertility or chronic pain.
Certain symptoms need urgent evaluation to prevent complications and reduce spread.
red_flags: Genital sores with significant pain or swelling
Blood in urine, severe burning, or inability to urinate
suggested_treatments: Avoid sex until evaluated and treated
Doctor-prescribed antibiotics/antivirals based on test results
questions_to_ask: What symptoms do you have (burning, discharge, sores)?

conditions.name: Infertility (Male/Female)
conditions.what_it_is: Difficulty achieving pregnancy after regular unprotected sex over time. Both male and female factors can contribute.
how_severe_can_it_be: Often treatable once the cause is identified.
Severity depends on underlying factors (hormones, ovulation, sperm count/motility, anatomy).
red_flags: Severe pelvic pain, irregular heavy bleeding, or known reproductive disorders
History of STIs, surgeries, or undescended testis
suggested_treatments: Initial evaluation: history + basic labs + semen analysis (for male partner)
Treat underlying hormone or infection issues if present
questions_to_ask: How long have you been trying?
Any known conditions (PCOS, thyroid, diabetes) or prior infections?
